accepted_lever: borrow root_jaxpr on the non-egraph dispatch path instead of cloning it

benchmark.command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-api --bench api_overhead -- 'api_overhead/jit/scalar_add|api_vs_dispatch/(dispatch_jit_add|api_jit_add)' --sample-size 20 --measurement-time 3 --warm-up-time 1

delta.score_rationale: Small but consistent fixed-overhead improvement on API-visible jit paths; low-effort one-line lifetime lever; direct dispatch also improves.

rejected_candidate.lever: reuse a static CPU backend registry with OnceLock
rejected_candidate.reason: direct dispatch regressed from 4.8641 us to 4.8939 us and API-level changes were too close to noise

behavior_proof.ordering: Non-egraph dispatch now borrows the same immutable root_jaxpr in the same sequence; egraph-enabled dispatch still owns the optimized Jaxpr before execution.
behavior_proof.tie_breaking: No ordering, sorting, or backend selection logic changed.
behavior_proof.floating_point: No primitive evaluation or AD arithmetic changed.
behavior_proof.rng: No RNG code or seed handling changed.
behavior_proof.strict_hardened: Compatibility mode, unknown-feature handling, cache-key construction, and backend fallback logic are unchanged.

validation.check: rch exec -- cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets passed; pre-existing fj-ad g_data warning emitted as warning only
validation.tests: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-dispatch -- --nocapture passed: 223 unit tests, 55 integration tests, doc-tests
validation.workspace_format: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check blocked by pre-existing formatting drift in fj-ad, fj-api, fj-conformance, and fj-lax
validation.clippy: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets -- -D warnings blocked by pre-existing frankenjax-ch8e fj-ad unused g_data warning
